method_atom: A many-body statistical model of a continuous multi-component vector order parameter with global rotational symmetry, simulated on a discrete grid under a small symmetry-breaking external field; two-point correlation functions of the components parallel and perpendicular to the field are computed and then inverted into frequency-domain spectral functions via a maximum-entropy reconstruction. This resolves how collective excitation modes evolve as a control parameter crosses a critical value — a sharp single-particle-like transverse (symmetry-restoring) mode versus a longitudinal response that develops a two-mode threshold at twice the transverse frequency plus a higher continuum — and tests a predicted convolution-type relation linking the parallel and perpendicular responses.
object_atom: The three-dimensional O(4) spin model: four-component unit vectors on a cubic lattice of linear size L=120 in a small external field H, studied above and below its critical temperature T_c, from which the transverse and longitudinal masses and critical exponents/amplitudes are extracted.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the symmetric multi-component order-parameter model plus maximum-entropy spectral reconstruction and the longitudinal-vs-transverse mode/convolution analysis; the O(4) four-component spins on a 3D lattice near T_c is the physics-specific object.
